data.title: Kodiak Gas Services announces stock sale by holders
data.description:
• Kodiak Gas Services (NYSE:KGS) has announced an offering of 5.5M shares of its common stock by an affiliate of the funds known as EQT Infrastructure III and EQT Infrastructure IV.
  • Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC and J.P. Morgan are acting as the underwriters for the offering.
  • Kodiak will not sell any shares of its common stock in the offering and will not receive any proceeds from the sale.
  • KGS shares dipped 2.16% premarket on Thursday.

data.title: Google's new chips, AI capabilities, could help cloud, ad businesses: BofA
data.description:
The launch of Google's (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) new tensor processing units and artificial intelligence models could help boost the tech giant's cloud and advertising businesses, Bank of America said on Thursday.

Shares of the tech giant slipped fractionally in premarket trading. However, they have gained more than 10% over the past five trading sessions, due in part to the announcement of its new quantum computing chip.

"Google’s proprietary new TPUs could be a differentiator for Cloud sales and will also help reduce internal compute costs," Bank of America analyst Justin Post, who has a Buy rating and $210 price target on Alphabet, wrote in a note.

The new Trillium chips — which were announced in May — are now generally available and offer four times the performance and are two-thirds more energy efficient than the previous TPUs, Google said.

In addition, the launch of its Gemini 2.0 Flash experimental artificial intelligence model should help the company's ad business by improving search and targeting, Post said. There's also the potential for new subscription offerings and enterprise offerings on the cloud side, thanks to the new models, he added.

"We acknowledge potential negative catalysts (ChatGPT product rollouts and potential ad tier, adverse remedies in Search case, or ruling in ad-tech case) but think Street could be underestimating potential upside from AI next year (AI Overviews, agents, cloud demand, etc.)," Post wrote in the note.

data.title: Gaxos.AI integrated Recraft AI into their AI solution
data.description:
• Gaxos.ai (NASDAQ:GXAI) has announced that the Co. successfully integrated Recraft AI into their artificial intelligence solution for game developers and publishers, Gaxos Labs.
  • “Game development is a labor-intensive process and the addition of tools that make the process simpler advances our goal of being the all-in-one artificial intelligence solution for game developers and publishers.” said Vadim Mats, CEO.

data.title: Allegiant Travel raises Q4 EPS outlook
data.description:
• Allegiant Travel (NASDAQ:ALGT) updated its Q4 EPS, excluding special charges, guidance to a range of $1.75 - $2.25, compared to the previous outlook of $0.00 - $1.00.
  • The consensus EPS estimate stands at $0.67.
  • ALGT shares were trading +1.50% Thursday pre-market at $88.00.
  • "Demand recovery to hurricane-impacted destinations has been faster than expected, with demand to most impacted regions returning to normal. Additionally, close-in holiday demand during November exceeded our expectations," said Chief Commercial Officer Drew Wells.
  • "December capacity is expected to be up 17 percent year-over-year, and we are encouraged by the strength in bookings for the December holiday period, which are outpacing our initial expectations," said Wells.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Lucid Group eyes partnerships amid the shifting EV landscape
data.description:
Lucid Group (NASDAQ:LCID) Chief Executive Officer Peter Rawlinson said in an interview on Bloomberg TV that the electric vehicle maker is ongoing conversations with a couple of automobile manufacturers about a partnership.

"It would be lovely if we could supply technology to a traditional car company to help them on their way to sustainability, and perhaps we can leverage economies of scale with their parts bin and other aspects of the business," stated Rawlinson.

Earlier in the year, Rawlinson said Lucid Group (LCID) was seeing increased interest in its technology.

Lucid Group (LCID) currently sells the Lucid Air and just started production of its Lucid Gravity SUV. The company has a contract to supply Aston Martin with EV components, but that launch has been delayed to 2026 due to a lack of customer interest.

The development is not a surprise since EV rival Tesla (TSLA) is flying high and Rivian Automotive (RIVN) has crucial partnerships with Amazon (AMZN) and Volkswagen (OTCPK:VLKAF).

Globally, Lucid Group (LCID) has a significant partnership with Saudi Arabia. In September 2023, Lucid (LCID) opened Saudi Arabia's first-ever car manufacturing facility, AMP-2, in King Abdullah Economic City. The plant initially has the capacity to produce 5,000 electric vehicles annually, with plans to expand to 155,000 units per year. Saudi Arabia has committed to purchasing up to 100,000 Lucid vehicles over a decade.

Shares of Lucid Group (LCID) rose 0.85% in premarket trading on Thursday.

data.title: MetLife targets adjusted ROE of 15%-17% in new long-term plan
data.description:
MetLife (NYSE:MET) targets adjusted return on equity of 15%-17%, adjusted EPS growth in double digits, free cash flow of $25B, and a 100-basis-point cut in its direct expense ratio target under its new five-year strategy to accelerate growth, the life insurer said Thursday.

By comparison, Q3 2024 adjusted ROE was 14.6%, Q3 adjusted EPS slipped 1.0% Y/Y, and its direct expense ratio, excluding total notable items related to direct expenses and PRT, was 11.7%. Free cash flow for full-year 2023 was $3.8B.

The company will unveil its "New Frontier" five-year strategy at its investor day. The plan seeks to:

  • Extend leadership in its Group Benefits business by enlarging the addressable market through more employers, more products per employee, and greater employee participation;
  • Capitalize on its retirement platform across its U.S. and Japan business through new liability origination and enhanced capital flexibility;
  • Accelerate growth in asset management by building on existing capabilities and broadening its line of investment products; and
  • Expand in high-growth international markets

MetLife (NYSE:MET) stock rose 0.4% in premarket trading.

data.title: Agnico Eagle Mines to acquire O3 Mining in C$204M
data.description:
Agnico Eagle Mines (NYSE:AEM) has agreed to acquire all of the outstanding common shares of Canadian gold explorer O3 Mining (OTCQX:OIIIF) at C$1.67 a share in cash.

The take-over bid is valued at approximately C$204M on a fully diluted in-the-money basis and represents a premium of 58% premium to O3 Mining's (TSXV:OIII:CA) closing price on Dec. 11, 2024.

The offer was unanimously recommended by board and special committee of O3 Mining and supported by shareholders representing 22% of its outstanding shares. It will be open for acceptance until 5:00 p.m. (Toronto time) on Jan. 23, 2025.

O3 Mining's primary asset is its 100%-owned Marban Alliance property located near Val d'Or, in the Abitibi region of Québec, and is adjacent to Agnico Eagle's Canadian Malartic complex.

Agnico Eagle's president and chief executive officer Ammar Al-Joundi commented: "Consistent with our regional strategy, this transaction is a tuck-in of the Marban deposit to our Canadian Malartic complex. The Marban deposit is expected to be complementary to other "Fill-the-Mill" opportunities at Canadian Malartic, further improving the production profile at a long-life world class asset.
